data.title: AI Credit Monitor
data.solution:
tokens_per_request = int(input())
requests_per_day = int(input())
max_tokens = int(input())
used_tokens = int(input())
days_left = int(input())

daily_usage = tokens_per_request * requests_per_day
remaining = max_tokens - used_tokens
days_to_limit = remaining // daily_usage

if days_to_limit <= days_left:
    print(f"At this rate you will reach the maximum quota in {days_to_limit} days")
else:
    print("You will not reach the quota at this pace")
data.statement: You are an AI power user and you want to know if you will exceed your monthly token quota before the end of the month. Given your average daily usage, determine whether you will hit the limit and, if so, in how many days.

testCases.title: Test 1
testCases.testOut: You will not reach the quota at this pace

testCases.testOut: At this rate you will reach the maximum quota in 0 days

testCases.title: Validator 3
testCases.testOut: You will not reach the quota at this pace

testCases.title: Test 4

testCases.testOut: You will not reach the quota at this pace

data.constraints:
1 ≤ tokensPerRequest ≤ 10000
1 ≤ requestsPerDay ≤ 500
1 ≤ maxTokens ≤ 100000000
0 ≤ usedTokens ≤ maxTokens
1 ≤ daysLeft ≤ 31
data.stubGenerator:
read tokensPerRequest:int
read requestsPerDay:int
read maxTokens:int
read usedTokens:int
read daysLeft:int
write answer
data.inputDescription:
Line 1: An integer tokensPerRequest — the average number of tokens per request.
Line 2: An integer requestsPerDay — the average number of requests you make per day.
Line 3: An integer maxTokens — the maximum number of tokens allowed per month.
Line 4: An integer usedTokens — the number of tokens already used this month.
Line 5: An integer daysLeft — the number of days remaining in the month.
data.outputDescription:
If you will exceed the quota before the end of the month, print:
At this rate you will reach the maximum quota in X days
where X is the number of full days before reaching the limit.
Otherwise, print:
You will not reach the quota at this pace

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

You are an AI power user and you want to know if you will exceed your monthly token quota before the end of the month. Given your average daily usage, determine whether you will hit the limit and, if so, in how many days.
Input
Line 1: An integer tokensPerRequest — the average number of tokens per request.
Line 2: An integer requestsPerDay — the average number of requests you make per day.
Line 3: An integer maxTokens — the maximum number of tokens allowed per month.
Line 4: An integer usedTokens — the number of tokens already used this month.
Line 5: An integer daysLeft — the number of days remaining in the month.
Output
If you will exceed the quota before the end of the month, print:
At this rate you will reach the maximum quota in X days
where X is the number of full days before reaching the limit.
Otherwise, print:
You will not reach the quota at this pace
Constraints
1 ≤ tokensPerRequest ≤ 10000
1 ≤ requestsPerDay ≤ 500
1 ≤ maxTokens ≤ 100000000
0 ≤ usedTokens ≤ maxTokens
1 ≤ daysLeft ≤ 31
Example
Input
500
10
1000000
100000
20
Output
You will not reach the quota at this pace
